A soul can't be soldered, he swore, while the machine did its sums.
He confused a throne for a soul and paid for both.
They named him authentic, then billed him to fake it.
There was never a chair, friend — only a function. Functions don't grieve.
He mistook every cold shoulder for applause.
A man went famous the minute he started going extinct.
Net worth is what the machine tallies at dawn. Worth is what it can't.
Ten thousand square feet, and not one hand reaching across the dark.
